what_it_does: Records a channel-memory attachment entry that links an item in the design-source channel to an image asset located at image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-08.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no additional body content
Fields include id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
Target uses an image:// URI pointing to presentation-15p/p-08
standouts: The target URI references a specific slide image p-08 inside a presentation-15p asset
It functions purely as a ledger/reference record rather than a content-bearing file
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Identifier follows an att- prefix pattern
Target uses a custom image:// URI scheme
Timestamp is ISO 8601 with explicit UTC offset
concerns: No title, caption, or description of the attached image is provided
The image:// target cannot be verified from the file alone
notes: This is a lightweight, timestamped attachment ledger entry pointing the design-source channel at a presentation slide image.
questions: What content is shown on presentation slide p-08?
Why was this particular image attached to the design-source channel at this time?
Is the referenced image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-08 asset currently available?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to be an attachment metadata record and it does exactly that: it records an attachment link to an image asset.
intention_signals: It seems intended to catalog and make discoverable a specific presentation slide image as an attachment associated with the design-source channel.
contribution: Provides a stable, timestamped pointer from a channel-memory attachment to a specific image asset, supporting asset discovery, provenance, and cross-referencing within the design-source channel.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment metadata file in the design-source channel that references the image asset image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-08 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record and expose a link between the design-source channel and a specific presentation slide image for retrieval and traceability.
